method: PyTorch FP-restore per-group sensitivity (proxy; --tensor-type unavailable so no real hybrid GGUF here)
finding: Single-group FP-restore is interaction-dominated (18/24 negative) on the un-adapted PTQ model; the additive-knapsack premise is weak. Clean positives concentrate at output-proximal layers (blk.11, blk.10) + a few early MLPs. Even the best single group recovers ~1 of the ~8.5 nat PTQ->FP gap, so bit-allocation alone cannot approach FP.
caveat: Proxy on the FP/PTQ model with FP-restore, not the ADAPTED model with real Q2_K. Faithful per-(group,choice) needs the adapted model + real hybrid GGUF (surgery).
